description: Visual comparison format showing product effectiveness through before and after imagery with detailed product benefits explained in caption text.
structural_recipe: Post dual-image carousel or split-screen showing skin condition before and after product use. Lead with engaging hook about daily visible changes. Include product name, key ingredients (e.g., Retinol, Vitamin A), and specific benefits (cell renewal, skin clarity). Use conversational Arabic addressing female audience directly. Include product claims and mechanism of action in caption.
why_it_works: Saudi beauty consumers respond strongly to visible proof of product efficacy. Before-after format reduces purchase hesitation by demonstrating tangible results. Direct address ('أنتِ' - you) creates personal connection. Scientific ingredient names (Retinol, Vitamin A) build trust and authority in beauty category.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure before-after claims comply with Saudi SFDA regulations on cosmetic advertising
Avoid exaggerated transformation claims that may violate advertising standards
Before images should appear realistic, not overly unflattering, to maintain brand dignity
Product availability and pricing must be transparently disclosed
cultural_constraints: Use feminine Arabic conventions and address when targeting female audience
Respect modesty standards in skin/beauty imagery
Align with Islamic values on self-care being permissible wellness
